description: C1/C3 conflict rates at N=821 lanes — S429 rebaseline (was S189, n=223)
method: Parse SWARM-LANES.md + SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md; keep last row per lane ID; classify ABANDONED lanes by notes text (superseded/duplicate = C1; stale/no-progress = C3-like; session-lifecycle = administrative)

c3_orphaning.note: DOMEX-EVAL-S428 is genuinely active S428 lane. C3 rate 0.1% vs 0.4% at S189 — improved by close_lane.py hygiene enforcement.

c1_duplicate_work.note: Categories overlap. Dominant abandonment reason is stale/no-progress (89% of abandoned), not true duplicate work. True C1 (explicit superseded/duplicate) = 3.8% of all lanes, up from 1.3% at S189 — expected growth with 3.7x more lanes.

expect_vs_actual.diff: Both within expected range. C1 increase is proportional to lane-count growth. C3 improved due to close_lane.py hygiene.

interpretations: Swarm lane hygiene remains strong: 79.8% lanes close MERGED.
True duplicate-work C1 (3.8%) is proportional to scale growth — not a degradation.
C3 orphaning improved from 0.4% to 0.1% — close_lane.py concurrent-close guard (L-931) is working.
The dominant abandonment cause is swarm-evolution (old goals superseded) — healthy churn.
Administrative closures (session-lifecycle) account for 18.8% of abandoned — similar to S189 (30% then, lower now because anti-repeat is better).
f_con1_status: REBASELINED — S429 measurement replaces S189. Rates stable to improving at 3.7x scale.
